title: To Shepherds As They Watched By Night

X: 71
T: To Shepherds As They Watched By Night
%%combinevoices 1
C: Words: Martin Luther, 1543. translated by Richard Massie, 1854. 
C: Music: 'Vom Himmel Hoch' traditional German from Schumann's Geistliche Lieder, Leipzig, 1539. 
C: Setting: "Common Service Book" (ULCA), 1917.
C: copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.

w: 5.~Not one He will or can for- sake Who Him his con- fi- dence doth make.

w: Made like your- selves of flesh and blood, Your Bro- ther is th'e- ter- * nal God. 
w: Let hell and Sa- tan rage and chafe, Christ is your Bro- ther ye * are safe. 
w: Let all his wiles the Temp- ter try, You may his ut- most pow'rs * de- fy.

% 11
W:  6.Ye shall and must at last prevail;
W:  God's own ye are, ye cannot fail.
W:  To God forever sing your praise
W:  With joy and patience all your days.

metadata.composer: 'Vom Himmel Hoch' traditional German from Schumann's Geistliche Lieder, Leipzig, 1539 "Common Service Book" (ULCA), 1917
metadata.lyricist: Martin Luther, 1543. translated by Richard Massie, 1854
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Martin Luther, 1543. translated by Richard Massie, 1854.
Music: 'Vom Himmel Hoch' traditional German from Schumann's Geistliche Lieder, Leipzig, 1539.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2009 Revision.

1.raw_text: To shepherds as they watched by night Appeared a host of angels bright; Behold the tender Babe, they said, In yonder lowly manger laid.

2.raw_text: At Bethlehem, in David's town, As Micah did of old make known; 'Tis Jesus Christ, your Lord and King, Who doth to all salvation bring.

3.raw_text: Oh, then rejoice that through His Son God is with sinners now at one; Made like yourselves of flesh and blood, Your Brother is th'eternal God.

4.raw_text: What harm can sin and death then do? The true God now abides with you. Let hell and Satan rage and chafe, Christ is your Brother ye are safe.

5.raw_text: Not one He will or can forsake Who Him his confidence doth make. Let all his wiles the Tempter try, You may his utmost pow'rs defy.
